title: euphorbia papillosa var. cabrerae
intro: Euphorbia papillosa var. cabrerae, also known as the Cabrera's Spurless Pencil Plant, is a succulent plant species belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine, with reported medicinal properties including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which are thought to be due to the presence of compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of Euphorbia papillosa var. cabrerae are said to include wound healing, pain relief, and the treatment of fever, as well as its use as an antiseptic to prevent infection. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tincture or infusate, typically made by steeping the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent, to harness its medicinal properties.
